value_accrual_summary: SUI stakers (validators and delegators) capture gas fees plus inflationary token emissions; there is no burn mechanism, so protocol value accrual depends almost entirely on emission-funded staking rewards rather than fee-driven deflation.

_notes.burn: Sui docs explicitly state there is no token burn. Gas fees are distributed to validators/stakers, not burned. Source: https://docs.sui.io/concepts/tokenomics/gas-in-sui
_notes.staking_apr: ~2.97% APR as of mid-2025 per CoinGecko staking data; this figure fluctuates and was NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER from official Sui docs directly — CoinGecko aggregates it from on-chain data. Treat as approximate.
_notes.inflation: Total supply is capped at 10 billion SUI, but a large portion of the supply is still being released from the treasury/reserve over time to fund staking rewards. The Sui tokenomics doc confirms staking rewards come from a combination of gas fees AND storage fund rebates AND token emissions from the reserve. Because emissions dominate over fee revenue at current network scale, staking_emission_inflationary=true.
_notes.fee_distribution: 100% of gas fees go to validators and their delegators (stakers) via the staking reward pool each epoch. There is no treasury cut or LP share. Reference: https://docs.sui.io/concepts/tokenomics/gas-in-sui
_notes.storage_fund: Sui has a unique 'storage fund' mechanism: users pre-pay storage fees which accumulate in a fund; validators earn a share of storage fund returns each epoch. This is a partial real-yield component, but it is small relative to emission rewards at current TVL/usage levels.
_notes.staking: SUI uses a delegated proof-of-stake model. Token holders delegate to validators; rewards are paid each epoch (~24 hours). Source: https://docs.sui.io/concepts/tokenomics/validators-staking
_notes.fee_capture: 100% of gas fees go to validators/stakers (no protocol treasury cut, no burn). Source: https://docs.sui.io/concepts/tokenomics/gas-in-sui
